--- title: "[summaries.tidy()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Return a standardised term-level DataFrame for a fitted model. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/summaries/_registry.py#L156-L192){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python summaries.tidy(model, **kwargs) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Produces one row per model term (coefficient, parameter, or stratum) with columns for the estimate, standard error, and confidence limits. This is the Python equivalent of R's `broom::tidy()`. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [model]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [object]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A fitted Greenwood estimator (e.g., `KaplanMeier`, `CoxPH`, `AFT`, `Parametric`). [**kwargs]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Forwarded to the registered adapter. Common options include `format=` to choose the output backend (`"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`). ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [DataFrame]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A tidy, term-level summary of the fitted model. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} Tidy a fitted Cox model into a Polars DataFrame: ::: {#e5a24528 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data, build a right-censored response, and fit a Cox model lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cox = gw.CoxPH().fit(y, covariates=lung[["age", "sex"]]) # Tidy the coefficients into a Polars DataFrame gw.tidy(cox, format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
shape: (2, 7)
termestimatestd_errorstatisticp_valueconf_lowconf_high

"sex"-0.5132190.167458-3.064760.002178-0.84143-0.185007
``` ::: :::
